content:
# Joshua Chapter 8 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 231-232 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_008_page_001.jpg`, `JOS_008_page_002.jpg`

---

**8:1** And-said YHWH to Joshua, "Do-not fear and-do-not be-dismayed. Take with-you all the-people of-the-war and-arise, go-up to-the-Ai. See, I-have-given into-your-hand the-king of-the-Ai and his-people and-his-city and-his-land.

**8:2** And-you-shall-do to-the-Ai and-to-her-king as you-did to-Jericho and-to-her-king; only her-spoil and-her-livestock you-shall-plunder for-yourselves. Set for-yourself an-ambush for-the-city from-behind-her."

**8:3** And-arose Joshua and-all the-people of-the-war to-go-up to-the-Ai. And-chose Joshua thirty thousand men, mighty-men of-valor, and-he-sent-them [by] night.

**8:4** And-he-commanded them, saying, "See, you [are] lying-in-ambush against the-city from-behind the-city. Do-not go-far-away from the-city exceedingly, and-be all-of-you prepared.

**8:5** And-I and-all the-people who [are] with-me will-approach to the-city, and-it-shall-be when they-come-out to-meet-us as-at-the-first, and-we-shall-flee before-them.

**8:6** And-they-shall-come-out after-us until we-have-drawn-them from the-city, for they-shall-say, 'They-[are]-fleeing before-us as-at-the-first.' And-we-shall-flee before-them.

**8:7** And-you shall-arise from the-ambush, and-you-shall-dispossess the-city. And-YHWH your-God will-give-it into-your-hand.

**8:8** And-it-shall-be when-you-seize the-city, you-shall-set the-city on-fire. According-to-the-word of-YHWH you-shall-do. See, I-have-commanded you."

**8:9** And-sent-them Joshua, and-they-went to the-ambush, and-they-sat between Bethel and-between the-Ai, from-[the]-west of-the-Ai. And-lodged Joshua that-night in-[the]-midst of-the-people.

**8:10** And-rose-early Joshua in-the-morning, and-he-mustered the-people, and-he-went-up, he and-[the]-elders of-Israel, before the-people to-the-Ai.

**8:11** And-all the-people of-the-war who [were] with-him went-up and-drew-near and-came before the-city, and-they-camped from-[the]-north of-the-Ai, and-the-valley [was] between-him and-between the-Ai.

**8:12** And-he-took about five thousand men and-he-set-them [as] an-ambush between Bethel and-between the-Ai, from-[the]-west of-the-city.

**8:13** And-they-set the-people, all the-camp which [was] from-[the]-north of-the-city, and its-rear from-[the]-west of-the-city. And-went Joshua that-night into-[the]-midst of-the-valley.

**8:14** And-it-was when-saw [the] king of-the-Ai, and-they-hurried and-rose-early, and-went-out [the] men of-the-city to-meet Israel for-the-battle, he and-all his-people, to-the-appointed-place before the-Arabah. And-he did-not know that [there was] an-ambush for-him from-behind the-city.

**8:15** And-were-struck Joshua and-all Israel before-them, and-they-fled [by the] way of-the-wilderness.

**8:16** And-were-called-together all the-people who [were] in-the-Ai to-pursue after-them. And-they-pursued after Joshua and-were-drawn-away from the-city.

**8:17** And-not was-left a-man in-the-Ai and-Bethel who did-not go-out after Israel. And-they-left the-city open and-pursued after Israel.

**8:18** And-said YHWH to Joshua, "Stretch-out the-javelin which [is] in-your-hand toward the-Ai, for into-your-hand I-will-give-it." And-stretched-out Joshua the-javelin which [was] in-his-hand toward the-city.

**8:19** And-the-ambush arose quickly from-its-place, and-they-ran when-he-stretched-out his-hand, and-they-entered the-city and-they-captured-it. And-they-hurried and-they-set the-city on-fire.

**8:20** And-turned [the] men of-the-Ai behind-them and-they-saw, and-behold, went-up [the] smoke of-the-city to-the-heavens, and-not was in-them power to-flee here or-there. And-the-people who-fled to-the-wilderness turned upon the-pursuer.

**8:21** And-Joshua and-all Israel saw that the-ambush had-captured the-city, and-that went-up [the] smoke of-the-city, and-they-turned-back and-struck the-men of-the-Ai.

**8:22** And-these went-out from the-city to-meet-them, and-they-were to-Israel in-the-midst, these from-this-[side] and-these from-this-[side]. And-they-struck them until not was-left to-him a-survivor or-an-escapee.

**8:23** And the-king of-the-Ai they-seized alive, and-they-brought-him-near to Joshua.

**8:24** And-it-was when-finished Israel to-kill all [the] inhabitants of-the-Ai in-the-field, in-the-wilderness where they-pursued-them, and-they-fell, all-of-them, by-[the]-edge of-[the]-sword until they-were-consumed, and-returned all Israel to-the-Ai, and-they-struck-it by-[the]-edge of-[the]-sword.

**8:25** And-it-was all who-fell on-that day, from-man and-unto woman, twelve thousand -- all [the] people of-the-Ai.

**8:26** And-Joshua did-not draw-back his-hand which he-stretched-out with-the-javelin until he-had-devoted all [the] inhabitants of-the-Ai.

**8:27** Only the-livestock and-the-spoil of-the-city that, Israel plundered for-themselves, according-to-the-word of-YHWH which He-commanded Joshua.

**8:28** And-burned Joshua the-Ai and-he-made-it a-mound of-perpetuity, a-desolation, unto this day.

**8:29** And the-king of-the-Ai he-hanged upon the-tree until [the] time of-the-evening. And-at-the-going-down of-the-sun, Joshua commanded, and-they-took-down his-corpse from the-tree and-they-cast it to [the] opening of-[the]-gate of-the-city, and-they-raised over-it a-heap of-stones great, unto this day.

**8:30** Then Joshua built an-altar to-YHWH, God of-Israel, in-Mount Ebal,

**8:31** as Moses, [the] servant of-YHWH, had-commanded the-sons of-Israel, as-it-is-written in-[the]-scroll of-[the]-Torah of-Moses: an-altar of-whole stones, upon which no-[one] had-wielded iron. And-they-offered upon-it burnt-offerings to-YHWH, and-they-sacrificed peace-offerings.

**8:32** And-he-wrote there upon the-stones a-copy of-[the]-Torah of-Moses which he-wrote before the-sons of-Israel.

**8:33** And-all Israel and-their-elders and-[the]-officers and-their-judges [were] standing from-this-[side] and-from-this-[side] of-the-Ark before the-priests, the-Levites, [the] bearers of-[the]-Ark of-[the]-Covenant of-YHWH, as-the-sojourner as-the-native-born, half-of-them toward [the] face of-Mount Gerizim and-half-of-them toward [the] face of-Mount Ebal, as Moses [the] servant of-YHWH had-commanded to-bless the-people of-Israel at-the-first.

**8:34** And-afterward he-read all [the] words of-the-Torah, the-blessing and-the-curse, according-to-all that-[is]-written in-[the]-scroll of-the-Torah.

**8:35** There-was not a-word from-all which Moses had-commanded, which Joshua did-not read before all [the] assembly of-Israel, and-the-women, and-the-little-ones, and-the-sojourner, the-[one]-walking in-their-midst.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **8:1 -- "the-Ai" (הָעַי):** The Hebrew consistently uses the article with Ai (ha-Ai), treating it as a common noun meaning "the ruin." EMET preserves the article throughout.

2. **8:3 -- "thirty thousand" vs. 8:12 "five thousand":** The MT reads שְׁלשִׁים אֶלֶף (thirty thousand) in v.3 and כַּחֲמֵשֶׁת אֲלָפִים (about five thousand) in v.12. The LXX (Vaticanus) reads τρισχιλίους (three thousand) in v.3, harmonizing with Joshua 7:4. This may represent an early textual variant or a separate ambush force. EMET follows the MT reading.

3. **8:9 -- "between Bethel and between the Ai":** The doubled בֵּין...וּבֵין construction is preserved literally. Hebrew expresses "between X and Y" as "between X and between Y."

4. **8:12 -- "about five thousand":** Hebrew כַּחֲמֵשֶׁת uses the prefixed כ (kaph) of approximation. This appears to describe either a second ambush party or a subset of the thirty thousand from v.3.

5. **8:13 -- "its rear" (עֲקֵבוֹ):** Literally "its heel," referring to the rear guard or western flank of the ambush position.

6. **8:14 -- "the appointed place" (הַמּוֹעָד):** From יעד, meaning the designated or customary meeting place, here before the Arabah (the Jordan rift valley).

7. **8:15 -- "And-were-struck" (וַיִּנָּגְעוּ):** The Niphal of נגע is used in the sense of feigning defeat. Joshua and Israel pretended to be beaten.

8. **8:17 -- "and Bethel":** The MT includes Bethel (וּבֵית אֵל) among those who emptied their city to pursue Israel. The LXX Vaticanus omits this reference. EMET follows the MT.

9. **8:18 -- "javelin" (כִּידוֹן):** The Hebrew כִּידוֹן denotes a short javelin or scimitar-like weapon, distinct from חֲנִית (spear). Some translations render "short sword," but "javelin" follows the context of an extended signal weapon.

10. **8:26 -- "devoted" (הֶחֱרִים):** The Hiphil of חרם, meaning to devote to destruction under the ban (cherem). This is holy war vocabulary indicating total consecration of the enemy to YHWH.

11. **8:28 -- "mound of perpetuity" (תֵּל עוֹלָם):** A tel is an archaeological mound formed by successive layers of settlement. The phrase means Ai became a permanent ruin-heap.

12. **8:29 -- Hanging and removal at sunset:** The practice of removing a hanged body before nightfall follows the command in Deuteronomy 21:22-23, preventing the land from being defiled.

13. **8:30-35 -- Altar at Mount Ebal:** The LXX Vaticanus places this episode after Joshua 9:2, which some scholars consider the original position. The MT places it here, immediately after the Ai conquest. EMET follows the MT order.

14. **8:31 -- "whole stones" (אֲבָנִים שְׁלֵמוֹת):** Uncut, undressed stones, as commanded in Exodus 20:25 and Deuteronomy 27:5-6. Iron tools would profane the altar.

15. **8:32 -- "a copy of the Torah of Moses" (מִשְׁנֵה תּוֹרַת מֹשֶׁה):** The word מִשְׁנֵה means "repetition" or "copy." This refers to the inscription of the Torah (or a portion of it) on plastered stones, as commanded in Deuteronomy 27:2-8.

16. **8:33 -- "as the sojourner as the native-born" (כַּגֵּר כָּאֶזְרָח):** Both resident aliens and native Israelites stood together for the covenant reading, indicating the inclusive nature of the assembly.
